name: Les Paul
description: American jazz guitarist, country guitarist, songwriter and inventor (1915–2009)
abstract: Lester William Polsfuss (June 9, 1915 – August 12, 2009), known as Les Paul, was an American jazz, country, and blues guitarist, songwriter, luthier, and inventor. He was one of the pioneers of the solid-body electric guitar, and his prototype, called the Log, served as inspiration for the Gibson Les Paul.

about_quotes.speaker: Rudy Van Gelder
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee (Rudy Van Gelder) does not speak in the provided transcript; the mention of Les Paul is made solely by the Interviewer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rudy Van Gelder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Nicki Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: I have to be in the mood or someone has to remind me of something to spur that on... watching Les Paul be the road... he was always good to me... sometimes he’d get mad because I went on the road. I couldn’t be there every Monday night for ten years
about_quotes.doc_title: Nicki Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe. Utica, New York, November 6, 2010 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Joel Dorn
about_quotes.evidence: Gene really knows what he's doing. He's Les Paul's kid. He grew up in the house where most of this was invented.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joel Dorn interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: I'm looking here, The Red Callender Six. That was with Herbie Haymer, Arnold Ross, Les Paul, Shadow Wilson.

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Les Paul touching the ground switch of our amplifier once at the house
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I went there and met Freddie Green, and that's -- at that time, Les Paul, I think it was -- Les? Yeah, I think it was Les ... he would come to the jam sessions someplace down in Morningside
